name: Front yard setback below minimum
message: The proposed front yard setback appears to be {{frontYardSetbackFt}} ft, but R1 zones ({{zone}}) generally require a 15 ft front yard (LAMC 12.08-C).
recommendation: Increase the front yard setback to at least 15 ft, or provide documentation of an approved adjustment, variance, or averaging determination.

name: Story count may exceed residential limit
message: The project proposes {{stories}} stories. Low-density residential zones are typically limited in height and stories (LAMC 12.21.1-A).
recommendation: Confirm the applicable height district and maximum stories for zone {{zone}} and revise the design or apply for the appropriate entitlement.

name: High-rise building triggers additional review
message: The project proposes {{stories}} stories and may qualify as a high-rise building, triggering additional structural, fire, and life-safety review (Title 24 Part 2).
recommendation: Coordinate early with LADBS and LAFD on high-rise requirements including smoke control, standpipes, emergency power, and fire command center.

name: Floor area or lot coverage may be exceeded
message: The proposed floor area of {{squareFootage}} sq ft is relatively large; verify compliance with residential floor area ratio and lot coverage limits (LAMC 12.21.1-B).
recommendation: Provide a floor area and lot coverage calculation demonstrating compliance for the parcel size in zone {{zone}}.

name: Hillside grading standards apply
message: The parcel is within a Hillside Area and the project involves grading. Hillside grading, slope, and access standards apply (LAMC 12.21-C.10, LAMC 91.7006).
recommendation: Provide a grading plan with cut/fill quantities, drainage and erosion control, and a soils/geology report, and confirm hillside access and floor area reductions.

name: Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone requirements

value: Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone

message: The parcel is within a Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone. Ignition-resistant construction, ember-resistant venting, and defensible space are required (LAMC 57.322, WUI standards).
recommendation: Detail Chapter 7A / WUI ignition-resistant materials, ember-resistant vents, and a brush clearance / defensible space plan, and coordinate LAFD brush clearance approval.

name: Methane mitigation required
message: The parcel is within a Methane Zone or Methane Buffer Zone. A methane mitigation system is required (LAMC 91.7104).
recommendation: Provide methane mitigation plans (membrane, sub-slab venting, gas detection) prepared by a qualified consultant and obtain LADBS Methane Mitigation review.

name: Soils report needed for liquefaction/landslide/hillside
message: The site is in a seismic hazard or hillside area (hazards: {{hazards}}). A geotechnical/soils report is generally required (LAMC 91.7006, 91.1613).
recommendation: Submit a geotechnical/soils report addressing liquefaction, landslide, or fault conditions and incorporate its recommendations into the foundation design.

name: Coastal Development Permit likely required
message: The parcel appears to be within the Coastal Zone. A Coastal Development Permit is generally required before a building permit can be issued (Pub. Res. Code 30600).
recommendation: Obtain a Coastal Development Permit (or Coastal Exemption) from City Planning and, where applicable, the California Coastal Commission prior to plan check clearance.

name: HPOZ historic review required
message: The parcel is within a Historic Preservation Overlay Zone (HPOZ). Exterior work, additions, new construction, or demolition require HPOZ design review (LAMC 12.20.3).
recommendation: Submit for a Certificate of Appropriateness or Certificate of Compatibility and demonstrate consistency with the HPOZ preservation plan.

name: Parking minimums may not apply near transit
message: The parcel is near major transit ({{overlays}}). Under AB 2097, minimum automobile parking requirements generally may not be imposed within one-half mile of a major transit stop.
recommendation: Confirm eligibility under AB 2097; you may reduce or eliminate off-street parking, though bicycle and accessible parking still apply.

name: TOC incentives available for affordable project
message: The parcel is in a TOC area ({{overlays}}) and the project includes affordable housing. Density bonus, FAR, height, and parking incentives may be available (LAMC 12.22-A.31).
recommendation: Consider filing a TOC application to obtain base incentives and additional on-menu/off-menu incentives commensurate with the affordability level.

name: Accessibility documentation missing for commercial work
message: This is a commercial project but no accessibility plans were identified. An accessible route, entrance, restrooms, and parking must be documented (CBC 11B-206, 11B-208, 11B-213).
recommendation: Provide accessibility plans showing the accessible path of travel, accessible entrance, accessible parking, and accessible restrooms per CBC Chapter 11B.

name: Multi-family accessibility requirements apply
message: The project has {{units}} dwelling units. Multi-family accessibility provisions (covered/adaptable units, accessible common areas, and path of travel) apply (CBC 11B / Chapter 11A).
recommendation: Identify the required number of accessible/adaptable units and provide accessible common-use areas and an accessible route throughout the site.

name: Large occupant load restroom/egress check
message: The stated occupant load is {{occupantLoad}}. Verify egress capacity, exit count, and accessible restroom fixture counts for the assembly/business occupancy (CBC Ch.10, 11B-213).
recommendation: Provide an occupant load and plumbing fixture calculation and confirm the number and arrangement of exits and accessible restrooms.

name: CALGreen mandatory measures for new construction
message: New construction must comply with CALGreen mandatory measures including water efficiency, construction waste diversion, and EV readiness (Title 24 Part 11).
recommendation: Provide a completed green code compliance document (green_code) and incorporate mandatory CALGreen measures into the plans.

name: Title 24 energy compliance expected
message: Work affecting the building envelope, HVAC, or lighting requires Title 24 energy compliance documentation (Title 24 Part 6).
recommendation: Provide Title 24 energy compliance forms (title24_energy) signed by the responsible party.

name: Occupancy classification required for commercial
message: No occupancy group classification was identified for this commercial project. Every building must be classified into an occupancy group (LAMC 91.403).
recommendation: State the occupancy group(s) (e.g., B, M, A-2) on the plans and confirm allowable area and required separations for the construction type.

name: Mixed-occupancy separation check
message: The project appears to be mixed-use. Fire-resistance-rated occupancy separations between occupancy groups may be required (CBC 508, Title 24 Part 2 Ch.7).
recommendation: Show occupancy separation ratings or justify nonseparated occupancies, and provide fire-rated assembly details.

name: Construction type and allowable area verification
message: At {{squareFootage}} sq ft, confirm the building's construction type supports the allowable height and area for the proposed occupancy (Title 24 Part 2 Ch.5).
recommendation: Provide an allowable area analysis including frontage and sprinkler increases and confirm the construction type on the plans.

name: Sign area exceeds typical limit
message: The proposed sign area is {{signAreaSqFt}} sq ft, which exceeds typical on-site sign area allowances and may require a sign adjustment or sign district approval (LAMC 14.4.4).
recommendation: Confirm the maximum permitted sign area for zone {{zone}}, reduce the sign area, or pursue a sign adjustment/sign district entitlement.

name: Digital or off-site sign restrictions
message: The sign appears to be digital or off-site. Such signs are generally prohibited outside designated sign districts (LAMC 14.4.4).
recommendation: Verify whether the parcel is within an approved sign district; otherwise revise to a permitted on-site static sign.

name: Change of use may trigger zoning and code review
message: The project involves a change of use. Verify the new use is permitted in zone {{zone}} and reassess parking, occupancy, accessibility, and CALGreen requirements (LAMC 12.14-C).
recommendation: Confirm use permissibility (or obtain a CUP), and provide updated occupancy, parking, and accessibility analyses for the new use.

name: Structural calculations expected
message: No structural calculations were identified for this new construction. Seismic and gravity design calculations are typically required (LAMC 91.1613).
recommendation: Provide stamped structural calculations and plans consistent with the adopted seismic design provisions.

name: EV charging readiness for new parking
message: New parking facilities with {{parkingSpaces}} spaces must provide EV-capable/EV-ready infrastructure and charging stations (CALGreen, LAMC 93.0413).
recommendation: Show EV-capable, EV-ready, and EVSE-installed spaces on the electrical and site plans per the applicable ratio.
